source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure Amazon ECS task definitions are tagged

input_row.source: CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure Amazon ECS task definitions are tagged
input_row.description: Ensure all Amazon ECS task definitions have resource tags to facilitate asset management, tracking, and compliance. Note: This recommendation assumes that only the latest active revision of a task definition is in use. If older revisions are in use, apply the audit and remediation procedures to those revisions as needed.
input_row.rationale: Consistent tagging supports compliance and helps identify unauthorized or misconfigured resources. Impact: There is minimal administrative overhead associated with implementing and maintaining resource tags.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Task definitions. 3. Click the name of a task definition. 4. Click on the latest active revision of the task definition. 5. Click Tags. 6. Ensure at least one tag is listed that does not begin with aws:. Tags prefixed with aws: are AWS-managed. 7. Repeat steps 1-6 for each ECS task definition. From Command Line: Run the following command to list task definitions: aws ecs list-task-definitions For the latest revision of a task definition, run the following command to view the tags: aws ecs list-tags-for-resource --resource-arn <task-definition-arn> Ensure that tags are returned that do not begin with aws:. Tags prefixed with aws: are AWS-managed. Repeat for each task definition.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to the ECS console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/ecs/. 2. In the left panel, click Task definitions. 3. Click the name of a task definition. 4. Click on the latest active revision of the task definition. 5. Click Create new revision. 6. Click Create new revision again. 7. Expand the Tags section. 8. Click Add tag. 9. Provide a Key and optional Value for the tag. 10. Click Create. 11. Repeat steps 1-10 for each task definition requiring remediation. Note: When a task definition is updated, running tasks launched from the previous task definition remain unchanged. Updating a running task requires redeploying it with the new task definition. Default Value: By default, Amazon ECS task definitions are not tagged. References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/ecs-using- tags.html 2. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- task-definitions.html 3. https://awscli.amazonaws.com/v2/documentation/api/latest/reference/ecs/list- tags-for-resource.html
input_row.impact: There is minimal administrative overhead associated with implementing and maintaining resource tags.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

- ✓ AWS APIs can query the tags for ECS task definitions, making this check automatable.
- ✓ Tagging is a standard AWS resource management property, which AWS APIs expose.
- ✓ Boto3 SDK has methods to check tags on ECS task definitions.
- ✓ The AWS CLI section provides specific commands to retrieve this information.
- ✓ The audit instructions mention using AWS CLI commands that can be automated.

Since all criteria indicate that the compliance check can be completed via AWS APIs or CLI, the process is suited for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_ecs_task_definition_tags_present

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- Use the AWS CLI or boto3 SDK to automate the task.
- **AWS CLI Commands:**
  - Execute `aws ecs list-task-definitions` to retrieve all ECS task definitions.
  - For each task definition, extract the latest revision using `aws ecs describe-task-definition --task-definition <task-definition-arn>`.
  - Use the `aws ecs list-tags-for-resource --resource-arn <task-definition-arn:revision>` command to check for tags.
  - Ensure the list of tags includes at least one tag not starting with `aws:`, confirming user-defined tags are present.
  
- **boto3 SDK Method:**
  - Use `boto3.client('ecs')` and its `list_task_definitions()` method to list all ECS task definitions.
  - Iterate over task definitions and use `describe_task_definition(taskDefinition=<task-definition-arn>)` to get details of the latest revision.
  - Call `list_tags_for_resource(resourceArn=<task-definition-arn:revision>)` to fetch tags.
  - Validate that non-`aws:`-prefixed tags exist to satisfy the tagging control.

- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass**: Each ECS task definition contains at least one tag that does not start with `aws:`.
  - **Fail**: Any ECS task definition lacks user-defined tags, indicating non-compliance.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI provide the necessary capabilities to automate the checking of tags on ECS task definitions. The use of these tools aligns with best practices for automating compliance checks.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9
- The automation details are technically accurate. The AWS CLI commands and boto3 methods mentioned are correct for retrieving and checking tags on ECS task definitions.
- The CLI commands `aws ecs list-task-definitions`, `aws ecs describe-task-definition`, and `aws ecs list-tags-for-resource` are valid and appropriate for this task.
- The boto3 methods `list_task_definitions()`, `describe_task_definition()`, and `list_tags_for_resource()` are correctly identified for use.
- The program name `aws_ecs_task_definition_tags_present` is descriptive and clear, though it could be slightly more specific by indicating the check is for user-defined tags.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `aws_ecs_task_definition_user_tags_present` to explicitly indicate that the focus is on user-defined tags, not just any tags.
- Consideration of AWS Config rules for continuous compliance monitoring could be mentioned. AWS Config can be used to ensure that ECS task definitions remain compliant over time.
- The audit could also include a check for older revisions if they are still in use, as mentioned in the control description.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, and the automation details are technically sound. The use of AWS APIs and CLI for this task is appropriate and aligns with AWS best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `aws_ecs_task_definition_user_tags_present` for clarity on the specific compliance check being performed.
- Consider mentioning AWS Config as an additional tool for ongoing compliance monitoring.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS ECS task definitions and their tags are fully accessible via AWS APIs (boto3, AWS CLI). The audit steps can be replicated programmatically by listing all ECS task definitions, retrieving their latest active revisions, and checking for user-defined tags (excluding those prefixed with aws:). This is a standard, automatable compliance check supported by AWS resource tagging APIs.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_ecs_task_definition_user_tags_present

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: Amazon ECS Task Definitions (latest active revisions)
- API/CLI Method: 
  - boto3: `ecs.list_task_definitions()`, `ecs.describe_task_definition()`, `ecs.list_tags_for_resource()`
  - AWS CLI: `aws ecs list-task-definitions`, `aws ecs describe-task-definition`, `aws ecs list-tags-for-resource`
- Properties to Check: 
  - For each latest active task definition revision, retrieve tags via `list-tags-for-resource` and ensure at least one tag exists that does NOT start with `aws:`.
- Pass Criteria: 
  - The latest active revision of each ECS task definition has at least one user-defined tag (i.e., a tag key not prefixed with `aws:`).
- Fail Criteria: 
  - The latest active revision of any ECS task definition has no tags, or only tags with keys starting with `aws:`.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation. The only minor difference was the suggested program name; I selected the more descriptive version (`aws_ecs_task_definition_user_tags_present`) to explicitly indicate the check is for user-defined tags, as recommended in the second opinion.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.1.0
